children.heading: Lease considered as sale
children.label: a Lease considered as sale
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, the lease of an article (including any renewal or any extension of a lease or any subsequent lease of such article) by the manufacturer, producer, or importer shall be considered a sale of such article.

children.heading: Limitation on tax
children.label: b Limitation on tax
children.text: In the case of any lease described in subsection (a) of an article taxable under this chapter, if the tax under this chapter is based on the price for which such articles are sold, there shall be paid on each lease payment with respect to such article a percentage of such payment equal to the rate of tax in effect on the date of such payment, until the total of the tax payments under such lease and any prior lease to which this subsection applies equals the total tax.

except as provided in paragraph (2), the tax computed on the constructive sale price for such article which would be determined under section 4216(b) if such article were sold at retail on the date of the first lease to which subsection (b) applies; or

if the first lease to which subsection (b) applies is not the first lease of the article, the tax computed on the fair market value of such article on the date of the first lease to which subsection (b) applies.

children.heading: Lessor must also be engaged in selling
children.label: 1 Lessor must also be engaged in selling
children.text: Subsection (b) shall not apply to any lease of an article unless at the time of making the lease, or any prior lease of such article to which subsection (b) applies, the person making the lease or prior lease was also engaged in the business of selling in arm’s length transactions the same type and model of article.

children.heading: Sale before total tax becomes payable
children.label: 2 Sale before total tax becomes payable
A
children.text: the difference between (i) the tax imposed on lease payments under leases of such article to which subsection (b) applies, and (ii) the total tax, or

children.label: B
children.text: a tax computed, at the rate in effect on the date of the sale, on the price for which the article is sold.

children.heading: Sale after total tax has become payable
children.label: 3 Sale after total tax has become payable
children.text: If the taxpayer sells an article after the total tax has become payable, no tax shall be imposed under this chapter on such sale.

children.heading: Leases of automobiles subject to gas guzzler tax
children.label: e Leases of automobiles subject to gas guzzler tax
A
children.text: the first lease of such automobile by the manufacturer shall be considered to be a sale, and

children.label: B
children.text: any lease of such automobile by the manufacturer after the first lease of such automobile shall not be considered to be a sale.

children.label: A
children.text: there shall be paid by the manufacturer on each lease payment that portion of the total gas guzzler tax which bears the same ratio to such total gas guzzler tax as such payment bears to the total amount to be paid under such lease,

children.label: B
children.text: if such lease is canceled, or the automobile is sold or otherwise disposed of, before the total gas guzzler tax is payable, there shall be paid by the manufacturer on such cancellation, sale, or disposition the difference between the tax imposed under subparagraph (A) on the lease payments and the total gas guzzler tax, and

children.label: C
children.text: if the automobile is sold or otherwise disposed of after the total gas guzzler tax is payable, no tax shall be imposed under section 4064 on such sale or disposition.

children.heading: Manufacturer
children.label: A Manufacturer
children.text: The term “manufacturer” includes a producer or importer.

children.heading: Total gas guzzler tax
children.label: B Total gas guzzler tax
children.text: The term “total gas guzzler tax” means the tax imposed by section 4064, computed at the rate in effect on the date of the first lease.
